headline: Saliva Test Cuts Endometriosis Diagnosis From Years to Days
meta_description: A new saliva-based test approved by the UK's NHS can diagnose endometriosis in days instead of the typical nine-year wait, offering hope to millions.
summary_short: Two rapid saliva tests called EndoSure and Endotest can diagnose endometriosis in days rather than the average nine-year wait, transforming care for patients.
body:
For millions of people living with endometriosis, getting a diagnosis has meant years of uncertainty, pain, and medical visits. A newly approved saliva test promises to change that dramatically. EndoSure and Endotest, developed specifically for rapid diagnosis, can identify the condition in days rather than the average nine-year journey many patients currently face.

The test has already been approved by the UK's National Health Service and is being celebrated by endometriosis specialists as genuinely transformative. By moving diagnosis from a lengthy process involving multiple consultations and procedures to a simple, non-invasive saliva sample, the tests could help people access treatment far sooner and reduce years of suffering.

This breakthrough matters beyond just speed. Faster diagnosis means people can begin proper treatment earlier, manage their symptoms more effectively, and reclaim quality of life that would otherwise be lost to the diagnostic wait. For a condition affecting millions globally, this represents a meaningful step forward in healthcare.
